originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: Keeping Everyone Safe: News from Chad and Sudan!
content:
Hello, little friends! Your friendly African news reporter is here to tell you about something happening between two countries in our wonderful continent: Chad and Sudan. They are neighbors, just like you might have a friend living in the house next door to you. Sometimes, even good neighbors can have a little problem, and that's what we're going to talk about today. But don't worry, grown-ups are working very hard to keep everyone safe and happy!

Our Neighbor Countries: Chad and Sudan

Imagine Africa, our big, beautiful continent, shaped like a friendly giant. Chad and Sudan are two countries right there, side by side. Chad is known for its amazing deserts and a special kind of bird called a bustard, while Sudan has the long, long River Nile flowing through it. They share a very long line on the ground called a border. A border is like an invisible fence that shows where one country ends and another begins. It helps everyone know which country they are in.

Something Sad Happened

Recently, something not very kind happened near this border between Chad and Sudan. Some people were together, feeling sad about something important, when suddenly, something flew from the sky and hit nearby. It was not a friendly thing at all. This made some people very, very sad, and a few people got hurt. It was a big problem, and it made many people worry.

President Déby Wants Safety First!

When something like this happens, the leaders of countries need to act quickly to protect their people. The President of Chad is called President Idris Mahamat Déby. He is like the head teacher or the head parent for the whole country of Chad. His most important job is to make sure everyone in Chad is safe and sound. When he heard about the sad event, President Déby made some very important decisions. He told his special helpers, the soldiers, to be extra, extra careful. He said, "Everyone must be on high alert!" This means they have to watch everything closely, like a superhero watching out for danger, to make sure nothing bad happens again.

Closing the Border: Like Closing a Door

President Déby also made another big decision: he said, "We need a total closure of the border!" Now, what does that mean? Imagine your mom or dad saying, "Let's close the front door and stay inside for a little while, because it's not safe outside right now." That's a bit like what closing the border means. It means that for a little while, it will be very difficult for people to travel between Chad and Sudan across that shared line. It's like putting up a big, strong, but invisible, 'Do Not Enter' sign for a short time. This is not to be unfriendly, but to keep people safe while the grown-ups figure out exactly what happened and how to make sure it doesn't happen again.

Did You Know?

Did you know that presidents in Africa, and all over the world, work very hard every day to make sure their countries are peaceful and their people are happy and safe? It's a very big job!

Why is this Important?

Closing the border and being on high alert helps to stop any more problems from happening. It gives the leaders, like President Déby, time to think and talk. Grown-ups, especially wise leaders, know that talking is always the best way to solve problems. They will talk to the leaders of Sudan to understand what happened and how they can be good neighbors again. It's like when you have a disagreement with a friend. Instead of shouting or pushing, you talk about it, right? Leaders do the same thing for countries. They want to find a peaceful way for Chad and Sudan to live side by side, as good friends and neighbors, just like always.

Hoping for Peace and Friendship

Everyone, especially the children of Chad and Sudan, wants peace. We hope that the leaders will talk and talk, and find a way to make everything friendly again. We want the borders to be safe and open again so people can visit and trade and be good neighbors. Remember, even when things are a little bit tricky, there are always good people, like President Déby and other leaders, working hard to make things better and keep everyone safe. We are all part of the big African family, and we always hope for friendship and peace between all our countries.

Did You Know?

Did you know that Africa has 54 different countries? Each one is special and full of wonderful people, animals, and places to explore! And many of them share borders, just like Chad and Sudan. So, for now, President Déby and his team are looking after Chad, making sure everyone is secure. They want to find a way to make sure such sad things don't happen again, and that Chad and Sudan can continue to be neighbors in peace and friendship.
summary: President Déby of Chad has ordered extra security and closed the border with Sudan after a sad event near the border, to keep everyone safe while leaders talk to solve the problem peacefully.
whyMatters: This is important because leaders are working hard to protect people and make sure countries can be good friends and neighbors, solving problems by talking instead of fighting.

originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
content: Caf executive committee member Augustin Senghor says the decision to strip Senegal of the 2025 Afcon title is "unacceptable, abject and we have to denounce it".

originalTitle: UK agrees deal to ease migrant returns to Nigeria
title: Our President's Big Trip: Good News from the UK!
content:
Hello, Super Star Kids!

Get ready for some super exciting news from far, far away! Guess what? Our wonderful President, President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, went on a very important trip. He travelled all the way from Nigeria to a country called the United Kingdom, or the UK for short! Imagine going on a big airplane ride across the sky! That's what President Tinubu did.

A Very Special Visit!

This wasn't just any trip; it was a special kind of visit called a 'state visit'. It's like when a very important friend comes to your house, and you make everything extra special for them! The President met with very important people in the UK, like their Prime Minister and even the King. When leaders from different countries meet, they talk about important things. Did you know? Our President's trip was extra, extra special! It was the first time in a super long time – even before your parents were born (37 years!), that a West African leader like our President went on such a big official visit to the UK. Wow, that's a long time!

What is the UK?

The UK is a country far away from Nigeria. It is made up of four countries: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland. It has big cities like London, and famous places like Buckingham Palace, where the King lives. Just like Nigeria has many beautiful cities and towns, the UK has its own special places too. People there speak English, just like many people here in Nigeria. Did you know? The UK has a very famous clock tower called Big Ben! It's super tall and rings beautiful chimes.

Why Did Our President Go?

Our President went to the UK for very important reasons. He went to talk to other leaders, make new friends for Nigeria, and find ways for our country to work even better with other countries. Think of it like you making new friends at school. You talk, you play, and you learn how to help each other. That's what our President was doing for Nigeria on his trip. He wanted to make sure Nigeria has strong friendships around the world. These friendships help us in many ways, like getting help with schools, hospitals, and even businesses. When countries are friends, they can share ideas and help each other grow.

Talking About Helping People

One very important thing they talked about was helping people. Sometimes, people from Nigeria go to live in other countries, like the UK. They might go to study at big universities, or to find work, or for other reasons. They might live there for a while, learn new things, and make new friends. But sometimes, these people want to come back home to Nigeria. Maybe they miss their families, or they want to help build our country with the new things they learned. Coming back home can sometimes be a bit tricky, but the President wanted to make it easier for our people.

A Special Promise for Our People

So, during his visit, our President and the leaders in the UK made a special promise, a 'deal'. This deal is all about making it easier and safer for Nigerians who are in the UK to come back home to Nigeria if they want to. It means they will get help and support to make their journey back smooth and happy. Imagine you went to a friend's house for a sleepover, and then it's time to go home. Your friend's parents help you pack up and make sure you get home safely and with a smile. That's a bit like what this deal is about – helping our people come back home to Nigeria with care. Did you know? An airplane can fly faster than a car, making it possible to travel across continents in just a few hours! It's like a giant bird flying in the sky!

Why is This Good News for Nigeria?

This is great news for everyone in Nigeria! When our people come back home, they bring back so many good things. They bring new ideas they learned from other countries, new skills that can help our businesses and communities, and lots of smiles to their families who missed them very much. They can help Nigeria grow even stronger and better! It also shows that Nigeria and the UK are good friends. When friends work together, amazing things can happen! They can help each other solve problems and make things better for everyone, not just in their own country, but all around the world. Our President wants Nigeria to have many friends and be a happy, strong country. Did you know? Nigeria is a very big country with over 200 million people! We have many different languages and cultures, but we all live together as one big family!

Working Together for a Brighter Future

This special visit and the promise made means that Nigeria and the UK will continue to be good friends and work together. It means our President is working hard to make sure all Nigerians, whether they are here or far away, are looked after and have a chance to come home if they wish. It's all about making life better for everyone in our beautiful country, Nigeria! So, let's cheer for our President and for all the good things that happen when countries work together like good friends!
summary: Our Nigerian President, Bola Ahmed Tinubu, went on a very special trip to the UK and made a promise with their leaders to help Nigerians who are there come back home easily and safely.
whyMatters: This is important because it means Nigeria is making good friends with other countries, and our President is making sure that Nigerians living far away can come back home to their families and help make our country even better and stronger.

originalTitle: US intelligence chief admits US, Israel not aligned on Iran war goals
title: US intelligence chief admits US, Israel not aligned on Iran war goals
content: The US Director of National ‌Intelligence says American and Israeli goals in the war on Iran are not the same.

originalTitle: Senegal and Morocco tied by religion and trade but divided by AFCON fallout
title: Senegal and Morocco tied by religion and trade but divided by AFCON fallout
content: Senegal were stripped of the AFCON crown with Morocco awarded the title, a move that divides two closely knit nations.

title: Netanyahu says Iran’s uranium enrichment abilities destroyed
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu claims Iran no longer has “the capacity to enrich uranium."

originalTitle: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
content: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

originalTitle: US considers lifting sanctions on some Iranian oil
title: US considers lifting sanctions on some Iranian oil
content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent broached waiving sanctions on oil already at sea, which would be a stunning reversal of longstanding American policy.

originalTitle: Denmark planned to blow up Greenland runways if US invaded, reports say
title: Denmark planned to blow up Greenland runways if US invaded, reports say
content: Denmark's public broadcaster reports that the US operation to seize Venezuela's leader sparked fears it would act against Greenland.

originalTitle: Carrick has no date for injured De Ligt's return
title: Carrick has no date for injured De Ligt's return
content: Manchester United defender Matthijs de Ligt is "improving" but manager Michael Carrick is unable to put a timeframe on the Dutchman's return from a back injury.

originalTitle: Uefa calls leagues to summit over 'microscopic' VAR
title: Uefa calls leagues to summit over 'microscopic' VAR
content: Europe's top leagues are summoned to a meeting with Uefa to discuss how VAR technology is being used.

originalTitle: Farewell number 18, hello number six - inside Man Utd's midfielder hunt
title: Farewell number 18, hello number six - inside Man Utd's midfielder hunt
content: Senior football correspondent Sami Mokbel breaks down Manchester United's search for a new midfielder to replace the outgoing Casemiro.
